ogTitle: Burnside vs Burpengary East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burnside and Burpengary East.
heading: Burnside vs Burpengary East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burnside, Queensland and Burpengary East, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnside has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Burpengary East (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burnside or Burpengary East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burnside has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Burpengary East scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burnside scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Burpengary East scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burnside or Burpengary East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnside has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
